message:
docs(cli,agents): document exit codes 10-24 + kebab-case kind canonical identifier [wan21]

Reality-check bead wan21 surfaced that cass emits 19 distinct exit codes
across src/lib.rs (1-7, 9, 10-15, 20-24) but only 0-9 were documented in
AGENTS.md + README.md + `cass robot-docs exit-codes`. Codes 10-15 and
20-24 were invisible to agents reading the published tables.

Mapping extracted by scanning CliError literals in src/lib.rs:
  10: config | timeout
  11: config
  12: source | ssh
  13: mapping | not_found
  14: io | mapping
  15: semantic-unavailable | embedder-unavailable
  20-21: model
  22: io
  23: download
  24: io

The numeric code is ambiguous at this level (e.g. 10 covers both
`config` and `timeout` kinds depending on context) — agents should
branch on `err.kind` from the JSON envelope, not on the numeric code.

Three docs updated to reflect this:

1. `cass robot-docs exit-codes` (src/lib.rs:6928-6939) — replaced the
   one-line 0-9 summary with three lines covering 0-24 plus a
   NOTE block explaining domain-specificity and the kebab-case kind
   discovery pattern. Agents running `cass robot-docs exit-codes --json`
   now see the full set.

2. AGENTS.md:574-601 — expanded the exit codes table from 10 rows (0-9)
   to 20 rows (0-9 + 10-15 + 20-24) with Retryable column preserved.
   Added closing paragraph pointing at err.kind as the canonical
   identifier plus a sample kind list.

3. README.md:780-805 — same table expansion + the same err.kind
   guidance, cross-referenced to the Error Handling section immediately
   above (which gwbt6 already fixed with the real error-envelope shape).

4. tests/golden/robot_docs/exit-codes.txt.golden — regenerated via
   UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --test golden_robot_docs
   robot_docs_exit_codes_matches_golden to match the new source output.

Verified: cargo test --test golden_robot_docs passes 4/5 (env,
exit-codes, paths, robot_help). The 5th (schemas) is unrelated pre-
existing drift from ibuuh.32's semantic-state schema work.

Refs coding_agent_session_search-wan21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -585,6 +585,18 @@ Returns in <50ms:
 | 7 | Lock/busy | Yes — retry later |
 | 8 | Partial result | Yes — increase timeout |
 | 9 | Unknown error | Maybe |
+| 10 | Config / timeout (domain-specific) | Depends on `err.kind` |
+| 11 | Config validation | No — fix config |
+| 12 | Source / SSH problem | Maybe — check remote host |
+| 13 | Mapping / not-found | Depends on `err.kind` |
+| 14 | I/O / mapping | Maybe |
+| 15 | Semantic / embedder unavailable | Yes — install model or fall back to `--mode lexical` |
+| 20-21 | Model acquisition failure | Maybe — check `err.kind`, `err.hint` |
+| 22 | I/O during model handling | Maybe |
+| 23 | Download failure | Yes — retry or use `--from-file` |
+| 24 | I/O during model verify/install | Maybe |
+
+**Codes ≥ 10 are domain-specific.** The numeric code alone is ambiguous (e.g. code 10 covers both `config` and `timeout` kinds). Agents should branch on `err.kind` from the JSON error envelope, not on the numeric code, when handling codes ≥ 10. Kind names are kebab-case (examples: `missing-index`, `missing-db`, `semantic-unavailable`, `embedder-unavailable`, `ambiguous-source`, `timeout`, `config`, `lock-busy`, `network`, `model`, `download`, `io`). The full set (~50 kinds) lives in `src/lib.rs`.
 
 ### Multi-Machine Search Setup

@@ -791,8 +791,18 @@ Errors are structured, actionable, and include recovery hints. A real sample fro
 | 7 | Lock/busy | Retry later |
 | 8 | Partial result | Increase `--timeout` or reduce scope |
 | 9 | Unknown error | Check `retryable` flag |
-
-Codes ≥ 10 cover domain-specific failures (semantic tier unavailable, analytics validation, export errors, etc.). Consult `err.kind` in the JSON envelope for the canonical identifier when `err.code ≥ 10`.
+| 10 | Config / timeout | Depends on `err.kind` |
+| 11 | Config validation | Fix config |
+| 12 | Source / SSH | Check remote host |
+| 13 | Mapping / not-found | Depends on `err.kind` |
+| 14 | I/O / mapping | Retry or inspect path |
+| 15 | Semantic / embedder unavailable | Install model or `--mode lexical` |
+| 20-21 | Model acquisition | Check `err.kind`, `err.hint` |
+| 22 | I/O during model handling | Retry |
+| 23 | Model download | Retry or use `--from-file` |
+| 24 | I/O during model verify/install | Retry |
+
+**Codes ≥ 10 are domain-specific** and the numeric value alone is ambiguous (e.g. code 10 maps to either `config` or `timeout` kinds depending on context). Agents should branch on `err.kind` from the JSON error envelope — not on the numeric code — when handling codes ≥ 10. See the Error Handling section above for the canonical `kind` list.
 
 The `retryable` field tells agents whether a retry might succeed (e.g., transient I/O) vs. guaranteed failure (e.g., invalid path).

         RobotTopic::Schemas => render_schema_docs(),
         RobotTopic::ExitCodes => vec![
             "exit-codes:".to_string(),
-            " 0 ok | 2 usage | 3 missing index/db | 4 network | 5 data-corrupt | 6 incompatible-version | 7 lock/busy | 8 partial | 9 unknown".to_string(),
+            " 0 ok | 1 health-failed | 2 usage | 3 missing index/db | 4 network | 5 data-corrupt | 6 incompatible-version | 7 lock/busy | 8 partial | 9 unknown".to_string(),
+            " 10 config|timeout | 11 config | 12 source|ssh | 13 mapping|not_found | 14 io|mapping | 15 semantic-unavailable|embedder-unavailable".to_string(),
+            " 20-21 model | 22 io | 23 download | 24 io".to_string(),
+            " NOTE: codes >= 10 cover domain-specific failures (sources/models/semantic/analytics).".to_string(),
+            "       Use `err.kind` from the JSON envelope as the canonical identifier — kinds are".to_string(),
+            "       kebab-case (e.g. missing-index, missing-db, semantic-unavailable, embedder-unavailable,".to_string(),
+            "       ambiguous-source, timeout, config, lock-busy, network, model, download, io).".to_string(),
+            "       Agents should branch on `err.kind`, not on numeric code, when handling codes >= 10.".to_string(),
         ],
         RobotTopic::Examples => vec![
             "examples:".to_string(),

@@ -1,2 +1,9 @@
 exit-codes:
- 0 ok | 2 usage | 3 missing index/db | 4 network | 5 data-corrupt | 6 incompatible-version | 7 lock/busy | 8 partial | 9 unknown
+ 0 ok | 1 health-failed | 2 usage | 3 missing index/db | 4 network | 5 data-corrupt | 6 incompatible-version | 7 lock/busy | 8 partial | 9 unknown
+ 10 config|timeout | 11 config | 12 source|ssh | 13 mapping|not_found | 14 io|mapping | 15 semantic-unavailable|embedder-unavailable
+ 20-21 model | 22 io | 23 download | 24 io
+ NOTE: codes >= 10 cover domain-specific failures (sources/models/semantic/analytics).
+       Use `err.kind` from the JSON envelope as the canonical identifier — kinds are
+       kebab-case (e.g. missing-index, missing-db, semantic-unavailable, embedder-unavailable,
+       ambiguous-source, timeout, config, lock-busy, network, model, download, io).
+       Agents should branch on `err.kind`, not on numeric code, when handling codes >= 10.
